title: Eden League Squash Tournament serves up excitement
description: GARDEN ROUTE SPORT NEWS - The Eden League...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE SPORT NEWS - The Eden League Squash Tournament for primary schools took place on 12 and 13 September, with matches hosted in George and Mossel Bay.


> Eight teams from six schools competed, including Bramble Hill International (George), Laerskool George-Suid, Outeniqua Primary (George), Vorentoe Primary (Great Brak River), Volschenk and Voorwaarts Primary (Riversdale).


A special highlight was Bramble Hill’s debut. Squash was only introduced as a sporting code at the school a few weeks ago, yet the learners managed to field a team after just seven practice sessions.

The competition was keenly contested, with Vorentoe A claiming gold, Outeniqua A taking silver and Voorwaarts securing bronze.

Vorentoe A (Arnoux Rossouw, Hanja Gildenhuys, Ethan van der Linde, Ruben Marx), Outeniqua A (Dante Brand, Luan van der Merwe, Deon Swanepoel, Markus van Zyl) and Voorwaarts (Erin Bothma, Alfred Robertson, Lohan Abrahams, Geovanni Peterson). Photos: Johnny Munro

Eight teams from six schools competed in the tournament.

The winning team: Vorentoe A’s Arnoux Rossouw, Hanja Gildenhuys, Ethan van der Linde and Ruben Marx.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
